fun_fact: Pre-monsoon showers are already lashing Kerala, where the southwest monsoon officially makes landfall around June 1. Over the next four months it brings ~70% of India's annual rainfall — recharging reservoirs, watering kharif crops like rice and groundnut, and shaping the trajectory of India's entire economy: a good monsoon can lift GDP, while a weak one drags growth, food prices and rural incomes with it.
